title: Quekettia theresae
taxonomy: Quekettia theresae is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Quekettia, which is a small group of epiphytic orchids native to Central and South America. The genus Quekettia is part of the subtribe Pleurothallidinae, which is a large group of terrestrial and epiphytic orchids. These orchids are known for their small size and intricate flower structures. Quekettia theresae is a rare and endangered species due to habitat loss and collection.

intro: Quekettia theresae, also known as the Quekettia orchid, is a species of flowering orchid native to the tropical rainforests of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Quekettia theresae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Quekettia theresae is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is commonly used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
